title: qPCR for Early Detection of Illegal Animal-Origin Ingredients
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Illegal     use     of     undeclared     animal-origin     ingredients     poses     significant     risks     to     food     safety  ,   public     health  ,   and     consumer     trust  .   Traditional     identification     methods  ,   including     microscopy     and     chemical     analysis  , are   time-consuming     and     often     lack     specificity  ,   delaying     enforcement     actions  .   Quantitative   PCR (  qPCR  )   enables     rapid  ,   sensitive  ,   and     specific     detection     of     animal   DNA in   food     products  ,   allowing   real-  time     monitoring     and     verification     of     compliance     with     labeling     and     regulatory     requirements  .   qPCR     is     especially     effective     for     complex     or     processed     foods     where     morphological     identification     is     impossible  . By   providing     results   in   just     hours  ,   qPCR     supports     early     intervention  ,   reduces     the     risk     of     food     fraud     reaching     consumers  ,   and     protects     vulnerable     populations  .   Integrating     qPCR     into     national     food     control     programs     strengthens     supply     chain     integrity     and     supports     international     trade     standards  .
